title: Cultural Differences: China vs Japan

left.title: Top-Down & Fast
left.description: Decisions made quickly by leaders. High flexibility to pivot and make course corrections.

right.title: Consensus Alignment
right.description: Bottom-up consensus takes longer, but execution is rapid and unified once decided.

left.title: Trial & Error
left.description: Deploy 80% solutions quickly, learn from the market, and iterate through feedback.

right.title: Risk Averse
right.description: Extensive testing and verification before launch protect brand reputation and trust.

left.title: Guanxi Personal Trust
left.description: Trust is built between individuals; personal connections help drive deals and bypass friction.

right.title: Organizational Trust
right.description: Trust builds between organizations over time, and loyalty to existing partners is paramount.

left.title: Flexible & Adaptive
left.description: Contracts are a snapshot; terms can be renegotiated if circumstances change.

right.title: Strict & Binding
right.description: Contracts are absolute, with meticulous adherence to specifications expected.

left.title: Pragmatic & Direct
left.description: High-context norms exist, but efficiency can override formality when results are at stake.

right.title: Indirect Context
right.description: Reading the room, avoiding open conflict, and preserving face are critical signals.

implicationText: Innovation requires distinct styles: agile pilots for China, rigorous validation for Japan.
